_schema.description: Project Kumquat curriculum matrix. The `curriculum` array is the primary dataset; every object is one study module and is safe to map directly onto an LMS node or UI card.

module_object_fields.phase_id: integer (1-3) — which of the three curriculum phases this module belongs to
module_object_fields.module_id: string — dotted identifier, e.g. '2.3', stable for deep-linking
module_object_fields.engineering_analogue: string — the Biology-as-a-Hardware-Stack framing for this module
module_object_fields.bilingual_keywords: array<{en:string, ar:string}> — EN/AR scientific term pairs
module_object_fields.resource_urls: array<{title:string, provider:string, url:string, type:string}> — verified external resources; UI should read `.url`

project.name: Project Kumquat
project.title: The Engineer's Blueprint for Veterinary Medicine & Stray Triage
project.philosophy: Biology-as-a-Hardware-Stack: organs are specialized sub-routines, the vascular network is fluid plumbing, and the immune system is a dynamic, multi-tier firewall. When a system goes down, we do not guess — we debug.
project.disclaimer: This curriculum does NOT replace a licensed Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM). It provides vet-level grounding for non-vets. Every triage path terminates at professional veterinary handoff. Nothing here authorizes diagnosis, prescription, or surgery.

phases.engineering_frame: The specification sheet — the known-good operating parameters and the tissue-level production plants of the body.
phases.summary: Establish healthy baselines and locate where each blood-cell line is manufactured and filtered. You cannot detect a fault without first knowing the spec sheet.

phases.engineering_frame: Root-cause analysis — reading the crash logs and constructing fault trees from ingress to systemic collapse.
phases.summary: Model how viral, bacterial, and toxic payloads execute code-injection attacks that drive cellular degradation, immune wipe, gut-barrier breach, and secondary sepsis with liver shutdown.

phases.phase_title_ar: الفرز الميداني وتصحيح الأخطاء أثناء التشغيل
phases.engineering_frame: Runtime debugging — a deterministic control loop executed live, under pressure, whose single exit condition is a clean handoff to a professional veterinarian.
phases.summary: Run a deterministic diagnostic loop on an animal in acute system failure: airway/breathing, thermal defense, capillary refill via the gum barrier, and fluid-collapse management before professional handoff.

curriculum.engineering_analogue: Top-level system block diagram of the body and its sub-routines.
learning_objectives: Draw a top-level block diagram of the major organ sub-routines and the plumbing that connects them.
Define the Biology-as-a-Hardware-Stack vocabulary used throughout the curriculum.
Explain homeostasis as the system's closed-loop control target.

resource_urls.title: EDIVET: Do you have what it takes to be a veterinarian?

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The body's primary manufacturing plant — the factory that builds the cellular workforce.
learning_objectives: Describe how bone marrow manufactures the three critical cell lines (RBCs, WBCs, platelets).
Explain why a high-mitosis core is a high-value target for cell-hijacking pathogens.
Relate the white-cell production rate to firewall (immune) capacity.

resource_urls.title: Overview of the Hematopoietic System in Animals

curriculum.engineering_analogue: A low-pressure return bus / internal message bus running parallel to the main plumbing.
learning_objectives: Trace the one-way flow of lymph from interstitial space back to circulation.
Contrast the low-pressure lymphatic return bus with the pressurized vascular loop.
Explain how the lymphatic network routes immune cells and antigens to checkpoints.

resource_urls.title: Overview of Leukocyte Disorders in Animals

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The firewall's inspection checkpoints — packet-filtering nodes on the transit bus.
learning_objectives: Locate the major lymph-node clusters and read a reactive node as a status flag on physical exam.
Describe antigen screening and the activation of an adaptive response at the node.
Explain why node enlargement signals active threat processing.

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The specification sheet — the known-good baseline readings against which all faults are measured.
learning_objectives: Recite normal feline baselines (temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, CRT, mucous-membrane color) from memory.
Note key canine baseline differences relevant to field work.
Identify a reading that has drifted out of specification.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Cornell Feline Health Center
resource_urls.provider: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine

resource_urls.title: EDIVET: Do you have what it takes to be a veterinarian?

curriculum.module_title: Threat Model: Viral, Bacterial & Toxic Payloads
curriculum.engineering_analogue: Enumerating the attack surface — three payload classes, each with a distinct attack signature.
learning_objectives: Classify an insult as viral (code injection), bacterial (resource hijack), or toxic (corrosive input).
Match each payload class to its characteristic damage pattern.
Explain why toxic payloads do not replicate while viral payloads do.

curriculum.module_title: Code-Injection Mechanics: How Pathogens Hijack Cells
curriculum.engineering_analogue: A malicious instruction set that commandeers host hardware to run and self-replicate.
learning_objectives: Describe the bind → inject → redirect → rupture cycle of a virus inside a host cell.
Explain why division-dependent viruses preferentially target high-mitosis cores.
Connect code-injection mechanics to the Phase 1 production plants at risk.

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The archetypal immune-wipe attack — the firewall's manufacturing plant taken offline.
learning_objectives: Explain how FPV targets bone marrow, intestinal crypts, and lymphoid tissue simultaneously.
Define panleukopenia as a collapse across all white-cell lines.
Summarize prevention through core vaccination per current guidelines.

resource_urls.title: Guideline for Feline Panleukopenia
resource_urls.provider: ABCD (European Advisory Board on Cat Diseases)

curriculum.engineering_analogue: A breached perimeter wall — intrusion across the I/O membrane exactly when the firewall is down.
learning_objectives: Explain how loss of rapidly renewing crypt cells breaches the gut perimeter wall.
Describe bacterial translocation into the bloodstream during immune wipe.
Link gut-barrier failure to downstream systemic infection.

curriculum.module_title: Cascade Failure: Systemic Sepsis & Liver Shutdown
curriculum.engineering_analogue: Uncontained fault propagation — a body-wide inflammatory storm that starves downstream organs.
learning_objectives: Trace bacterial translocation to systemic sepsis and distributive shock.
Explain endotoxin overload of the liver detox daemon and its shutdown.
Describe DIC as consumption of the platelet leak-sealing subsystem.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Overview of Fluid Therapy in Animals
Overview of Leukocyte Disorders in Animals

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The master control flow — an ordered, gated loop where each check gates the next.
learning_objectives: Execute the A → B → T → C → F loop in strict order without skipping ahead.
Explain why each check gates the next, mirroring an airway-first protocol.
State the single exit condition: a clean handoff to a professional veterinarian.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients

curriculum.engineering_analogue: The I/O gas-exchange subsystem — verify the intake path before anything downstream.
learning_objectives: Assess airway patency and clear a visible obstruction safely.
Judge whether breathing is present and effective, and when to escalate.
Recognize cyanosis as an oxygenation-failure flag requiring immediate action.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Trauma in Emergency Medicine in Small Animals

curriculum.engineering_analogue: Thermal throttling — a system drifting out of safe operating temperature.
learning_objectives: Recognize hypothermia as the common collapsed-kitten failure mode.
Apply gradual external warming and avoid aggressive re-warming faults.
Withhold feeding from a hypothermic animal whose gut has shut down.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Cornell Feline Health Center
resource_urls.provider: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine

curriculum.module_title: Perfusion Debug: Capillary Refill Time via Gum Barriers
curriculum.engineering_analogue: Reading the status LED — the gum is a direct readout of circulatory plumbing pressure.
learning_objectives: Perform the gum blanch test and time refill against the <2-second baseline.
Map mucous-membrane color (pale, blue, brick-red, yellow) to system interpretation.
Decide when poor perfusion mandates shock management.

resource_urls.title: Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Overview of Fluid Therapy in Animals
Cornell Feline Health Center
resource_urls.provider: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine

curriculum.module_title: Fluid Collapse & Shock Management to Professional Handoff
curriculum.engineering_analogue: Emergency power management — stabilize a collapsing system long enough to reach the real fix.
learning_objectives: Estimate dehydration percentage and compute the fluid deficit for the receiving veterinarian.
Recognize shock and conserve heat while preventing further fluid loss.
Produce a structured handoff report: baselines, deviations, interventions, computed deficit.

resource_urls.title: Overview of Fluid Therapy in Animals
Initial Triage and Resuscitation of Small Animal Emergency Patients
Feline Infectious Enteritis (Supportive Care & IV Fluids)

The Truth About Cats and Dogs
